blocks.label: Psychic Spear
blocks.value: Action, Range (20), Status (Quick), Psychic 2 AP, 4 END 2d6+POW Make a ranged attack roll against a target within range. On a hit, the target must make a GRI save or believe they have taken a Mortal Wound. They must make a Mortal Wound roll, and suffer from its effects until the end of their next turn. This Ability deals 1d6 extra damage per stack of Subjugation the target has. On three or more stacks, this Ability gains Armor Piercing and ignores Resistance. A character may only be affected by this Ability's secondary effect once per encounter. On subsequent uses, the target becomes Slowed on a failed GRI save instead.

blocks.label: Under My Authority
blocks.value: Action, Status (Quick), Displacement, Psychic, Armor Piercing 3 AP, 3 END 3d6+POW Force a target within line of sight to make a GRI save. On a failure, you may impose one of the following effects upon them. The target must then choose between taking the effect, or immediately taking damage. Puppeteer. The target moves a number of spaces up to their Movement to move in a direction of your choosing. This movement provokes reactions, and you may move the target into Dangerous Terrain or off ledges. If the target has two or more Subjugation stacks, you may force them to move a number of spaces up to twice their Movement. Compelled Attack. The target must immediately attack a random adjacent character with a damaging Ability of their choosing. If they have no valid Abilities, then they do a Basic Attack instead. If the target has two or more Subjugation stacks, you may decide which Ability they use. Disarm. The target has +2 Disadvantage on their first attack roll on their next turn. If the target has two or more Subjugation stacks, they also become Vulnerable until the end of their next turn. This secondary effect can only affect a character once per encounter.

blocks.label: Shackle of the Mind
blocks.value: Action, Displacement 1 AP, 4 END Force a target within line of sight to make a GRI save. On a failure, mark a space adjacent to the target. Until the end of the encounter, you may teleport the target to this space as a reaction, which can be taken once per round. If the target has two or more stacks of Subjugation, being teleported in this way also deals 1d6+POW Psychic damage. This effect lasts until you end it on your turn at 0 AP cost, or until the target reattempts the save at the cost of 2 AP and succeeds.

blocks.label: Instinct Override
blocks.value: Reaction 5 END When a character is targeted by an attack, you may use this reaction to force them to Defend, regardless of if the attack would actually hit them. You cannot force a character to Defend if they already Defended in the previous round.

blocks.label: Conqueror's Shroud
blocks.value: Passive, Status (Conditional) Characters with two or more stacks of Subjugation always treat you as Invisible when making attacks. If they have four stacks of Subjugation, you cease to be a valid target for any of their attacks and Abilities, though you are still affected by Abilities with the Area of Effect trait.

blocks.label: Physical Description
blocks.value: With a slight frame and plain features, Nacht would be wholly unassuming save for the black face mask perpetually covering their mouth. Nobody has ever seen them take it off, even to eat. Their face rarely shows emotion, the red flash in their eyes every time they cast a spell being the only indication of life in their vacant expression.

blocks.label: Backstory
blocks.value:
One of the many unwilling participants of the Institute's Artificial Mutant program, Nacht lives with induced mutations that render them unable to speak. This, alongside their strange magic and affinity for dangerous exotic animals, has made them a social outcast. Anyone who has gotten the chance to know her, however, will see that the only scary thing about her is her brutal honesty.

As a result of experimentation performed on them, Nacht's memories past their awakening as a mutant have been erased from their mind. All they know is the stark white light of the operating room, the feeling of a stiff hospital bed mattress against their back, and the dread of being mutilated only to be cast aside like garbage.

blocks.label: Innate: Serpent's Decree
blocks.value: Unlike other grimoires, Serpent's Decree does not have a physical form. It is etched into Nacht's body, the hexigraphs burned into their mind like parasitic thoughts that have made them forget the idea of silence. Because of this, they have the ability to shape their spells without uttering a single evocation, at the cost of untold mental strain. In the hands of a normal human, Serpent's Decree allows one to imbue their words with an unnatural persuasive power that controls the minds of anyone that hear them, but only after a lengthy incantation. Thanks to Nacht's mutations, however, they can psychically transmit their will directly into the mind of their target, without the latency of having to perform an incantation beforehand. This makes it appear as though Nacht's words themselves carry the power of Serpent's Decree, making her a terrifying foe for anyone without the mental fortitude to resist her influence. Insidious Coil Action, Status (Permanent), 1/round 0 AP, 3 END After using a Slotted Ability against a target, you may make a contested GRI roll against that same target. On a success, the target is given one stack of Subjugation, up to a maximum of four stacks. You make this roll with +1 Disadvantage for each stack of Subjugation the target currently has. For example, if the target has three stacks of Subjugation, you make the contested GRI roll with +3 Disadvantage.

blocks.label: Fatebreaker: Submit
blocks.value: Action 2 FP, 3 AP, 5 END Once a character has reached enough stacks of Subjugation, you may use this Ability to immediately force one of the following effects on them. Afterwards, all stacks of Subjugation on the target are cleared. Forbid (2 Stacks). Choose one of the target's Slotted Abilities. Until the end of the encounter, they cannot use this Ability or benefit from in any way. Obey (3 Stacks). You have control of the target's actions during their next available turn, which you can force them to take at any point in the round that you choose. While you are controlling them, you may move and perform actions as if you were taking your own turn. You cannot, however, take any actions that would cause direct harm to the target. Suicide (4 Stacks). The target immediately suffers one Mortal Wound of your choosing. This effect cannot be avoided in any way, even with Abilities that would otherwise prevent Mortal Wounds. If the target can no longer take any Mortal Wounds, you have the choice to make them Knocked Out or immediately kill them.
